title: To direct the Joint Committee on the Library to procure a statue of Benjamin Franklin for placement in the Capitol.
stage_label: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
plain_summary: This bill requires a group of lawmakers called the Joint Committee on the Library to get a statue of Benjamin Franklin made. They must sign a contract to have the statue created by the end of 2025. The statue must be placed somewhere in the Capitol building by the end of 2026 where visitors can see it during tours. The statue will be a permanent addition to the Capitol that people can visit.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.

sections.title: Section 1 Procurement and Placement of Statue of Benjamin Franklin in the United States Capitol
sections.text: The Joint Committee on the Library (a group of lawmakers who manage the Library of Congress) must get a statue of Benjamin Franklin by December thirty first, two thousand twenty five. They can make any deal they think is right as long as it follows the law. By December thirty first, two thousand twenty six, the Joint Committee must put the statue in a good permanent spot inside the United States Capitol building. The statue must be in a place where people can see it when they take guided tours of the Capitol that are given by the Capitol Visitor Center.
